# Versiontag

## Python project versioning for lazy people

{{ license }}

 

{{ kit }}

 

{{ format }}

 

{{ downloads }}

 

{{ travisci }}

## What's versiontag?

Versiontag is an ultra-simple library design to make accessing the current
version number of your software easy.

## Why do I need it?

Version numbers are all too often duplicated among setup.py, git tags, and
other sources of truth. This library makes it possible to consolidate on git
tags as a single source of truth regarding version numbers. That decreases the
likely hood of making errors when publishing new package versions.

**Full Documentation**: <https://python-versiontag.readthedocs.io>

classes.name: versiontag.tests.utils.TemporaryDirectory
documentation:
Create and return a temporary directory. This has the same behavior
as mkdtemp but can be used as a context manager. For example:

    with TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        ...

Upon exiting the context, the directory and everything contained
in it are removed.

methods.name: versiontag.tests.test_versiontag.VersionTagTest.test_head_is_post_release
documentation: Subsequent commits show as post releases

methods.name: versiontag.tests.test_versiontag.VersionTagTest.test_caching_with_removed_git_folder
documentation: Caching continues to return release even if git repository disappears

functions.name: versiontag.__get_git_tag
documentation:
Read the Git project version by running `git describe --tags` in the current-working-directory.

```
return

Project version string
```

functions.name: versiontag.__get_cache_file
documentation:
Get the path to the version cache file.

```
return

File path string
```

functions.name: versiontag.__open_cache_file
documentation:
Open the version cache file in the given mode and return the file object.

```
param mode

Mode to open file. See Python file modes.

return

File object
```

functions.name: versiontag.cache_git_tag
documentation:
Try to read the current version from git and, if read successfully, cache it into the version cache file. If
the git folder doesn't exist or if git isn't installed, this is a no-op. I.E. it won't blank out a
pre-existing version cache file upon failure.

```
return

Project version string
```

functions.name: versiontag.convert_to_pypi_version
documentation:
Convert a git tag version string into something compatible with [PEP-440](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/).

```
param version

The input version string, normally directly out of git describe.

return

PEP-440 version string
```

Usage:

```
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1')  # Normal Releases
1.0.1
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1-dev1')  # Dev Releases
1.0.1.dev1
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1-a1')  # Alpha Releases
1.0.1a1
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1-b4')  # Beta Releases
1.0.1b4
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1-rc2')  # RC Releases
1.0.1rc2
>>> convert_to_pypi_version('r1.0.1-12-geaea7b6')  # Post Releases
1.0.1.post12
```

functions.name: versiontag.get_version
documentation:
Get the project version string.

Returns the most-accurate-possible version string for the current project.
This order of preference this is:

1. The actual output of `git describe --tags`
2. The contents of the version cache file
3. The default version, `r0.0.0`

```
param pypi

Default False. When True, returns a PEP-440 compatible version string.

return

Project version string
```
